grants.title: Organisation of the process and EIT Awards Ceremony in Ireland (2026 december)
grants.description:
With this call for tenders, the EIT wants to buy the various services needed to organise the EIT Awards 2026 process and ceremony at the Irish presidency event in Limerick, on 3 December 2026.
For this, the EIT requests the following high-quality services:
Ensure the timely preparation and the delivery of all specified aspects of the process and ceremony, including communications, based on the concept developed by the EIT.
Support the organisation of the pitch training for the 2026 EIT Awards nominees.
Support for the event follow-up.

grants.title: Cross KIC EIT Innovation & Business Creation Business Plan 2026
grants.description:
Your role as a host

(1) Ignite NEB event (Ideation + Celebration)

A local, inspiring, 2+ day hackathon-style experience including:

  1. NEB Celebration (art, culture, performances)
  2. Ideation workshops (challenge → solution → pitch)
  3. Participants (citizens & change makers, challenge owners (e.g. city, NGOs, industry), coaches & mentors)
  4. Pre-event sessions (training, team formation, challenge definition)
  5. Jury selects two winning teams/ideas
  6. Should be delivered in September/October 2026

(2) 8-month incubation programme

Supporting winning teams from the Ignite NEB event to:

  1. Develop their ideas
  2. Build and test prototypes
  3. Validate or co-design solutions with real users
  4. Develop business or implementation models
  5. Engage stakeholders and communities
  6. Create new SMEs, start-ups, initiatives

grants.title: NEB Innovative Funding Advisory Hub
grants.description: The European Commission, in line with the New European Bauhaus (NEB) initiative, aims to create an interactive IT platform called the ‘NEB Funding Hub’. This platform will facilitate the matchmaking of small-scale projects that align with the core NEB values (sustainability, inclusion, and quality of experience) with appropriate funding sources, including public and private financiers, as well as crowdfunding entities.  The platform will support the identification, ranking, and financing of NEB-related projects with a funding requirement of up to an estimative value of EUR 100,000. The overarching goal of the assignment is to develop, launch, and maintain the NEB Funding Hub - an interactive online platform that brings together small NEB projects and funding providers (both philanthropic and other public/private financial entities). The platform aims to match small but high-potential NEB projects with funding opportunities through a tailored methodology for project matchmaking.

grants.title: Technical Assistance for Turkiye in Horizon Europe
grants.description: Integration of Turkish Research Area (TARAL) into European Research Area (ERA) by increased participation in Framework Programmes is a critical pillar supporting innovation and technology development in Türkiye. Further to Türkiye’s previous participation in EU programmes, there is still need to increase awareness, explore funding mechanisms and increase project applications in EU programmes among potential beneficiaries. Within the scope of the project, Türkiye’s performance in H2020 will be analysed through current situation assessment and roadmaps will be developed to increase participation in Horizon Europe.  According to results of assessment and roadmaps, capacity development trainings will be provided for the target groups, which will play an active role for the improvement of the R&D sector and ecosystem in Türkiye. Also, several knowledge diffusion and networking activities will be organized to increase awareness on Horizon Europe.
grants.search_text: technical assistance for turkiye in horizon europe integration of turkish research area (taral) into european research area (era) by increased participation in framework programmes is a critical pillar supporting innovation and technology development in türkiye. further to türkiye’s previous participation in eu programmes, there is still need to increase awareness, explore funding mechanisms and increase project applications in eu programmes among potential beneficiaries. within the scope of the project, türkiye’s performance in h2020 will be analysed through current situation assessment and roadmaps will be developed to increase participation in horizon europe.  according to results of assessment and roadmaps, capacity development trainings will be provided for the target groups, which will play an active role for the improvement of the r&d sector and ecosystem in türkiye. also, several knowledge diffusion and networking activities will be organized to increase awareness on horizon europe.

grants.title: Joint Cluster Initiatives (EUROCLUSTERS) for Europe’s recovery
grants.description:
Applicants are invited to submit proposals for collaborative innovation projects specifically focused on the Cultural Heritage Value Chain (CHVC). Projects must contribute to European strategic autonomy and the twin transition (green and digital).

Proposals must align with one of the following strand:

  1. Strand 1: Developing new-to-the-firm products or services.
  2. Strand 2: Adopting new-to-the-firm business processes.

For further information, please check the call document.

Key Requirements:

  1. Consortium: The Step 2 proposal must be submitted by a consortium of at least two SMEs from eligible SMP countries.
  2. Technology: Projects must demonstrate the application of EU-based digital solutions/technologies and/or the engagement of Innovation Leaders.
  3. Duration: The project implementation period is fixed at 12 months. If selected under Step 1, the SMEs will have to complete a set of mandatory activities (between May-June 2026) to be considered eligible to apply under Step 2.
  4. Projects require a minimum 15% co-financing contribution from the applicants based on the total project budget (e.g., a grant of €77,000 or €120,000 plus the mandatory 15% contribution). This contribution is mandatory for all participating SMEs within a project. While 15% is the minimum requirement, providing a higher percentage of co-financing will result in additional points during the evaluation process.


Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Applicants can consult Frequently Asked Questions at the following link.

Applicants are advised to consult them regularly, as the document is continuously updated.

grants.title: Inter-institutional single framework contract to support the monitoring of LIFE projects (action and operating grants), communication about the LIFE programme and other related activities
grants.description:
CINEA and DG ENV require substantial external support in the following areas:

1. Monitoring of LIFE projects, including action grants and operating grants: around 1 000 grants are to be monitored across a wide thematic and geographical coverage. There is a need for an external team of monitors covering the different thematic areas and possessing knowledge of the relevant national or local contexts and languages, in order to ensure proper monitoring of the projects and to provide support where issues arise.

2. Communication on the LIFE programme and project achievements, with a view to outreach and potential replication: this includes, for example, social media, Info Days, the LIFE Awards, other ad hoc events, participation in third-party events, project news, newsletters, and publications. It should be emphasised that LIFE grants support actions at local, regional, and national level that are close to citizens and generate tangible environmental, climate and health benefits. There is therefore a significant volume of material and strong potential to promote a positive image of the EU.

3. Other related activities include policy feedback events and studies or activities, whether online or in-person, upon request from the parent DGs, namely ENV, CLIMA and ENER; annual Welcome Meetings for new projects; and support to projects aimed at facilitating commercial success.

Activities will take place in the EU, in third countries associated with the LIFE Programme: https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/docs/2021-2027/life/guidance/list3rdcountryparticipation_life_en.pdf and, very rarely, in overseas countries and territories, with fewer than 10 project visits estimated over the duration of the framework contract: https://international-partnerships.ec.europa.eu/countries/overseas-countries-and-territories_en

grants.title: Specialiserede uddannelsesprogrammer på centrale kapacitetsområder
grants.description:
Juridisk ansvarsfraskrivelse:

Denne indkaldelse (samt den finansielle støtte, der vil blive ydet gennem den) er IKKE en EU-foranstaltning. Denne indkaldelse iværksættes udelukkende af ACHIEVE. Den finansielle støtte til de udvalgte udvalgte ansøgere ydes direkte af ACHIEVE i eget navn og på eget ansvar. ACHIEVE'seget ansvar omfatter forberedelse og udarbejdelse af denne indkaldelse samt udvælgelse af udvalgte ansøgere, der vil modtage den nævnte finansielle støtte. Offentliggørelsen af denne indkaldelse på EU's Funding & Tenders Portal sker udelukkende til orientering med det formål at øge synligheden af denne indkaldelse for den brede offentlighed, herunder for potentielle ansøgere (dvs. studerende). Denne offentliggørelse må ikke fortolkes som en direkte eller indirekte godkendelse af indholdet af og betingelserne for denne indkaldelse fra de relevante EU-myndigheders side eller som nogen form for fritagelse fra ACHIEVE's ovenfor beskrevne ansvar.

Databeskyttelse/GDPR 

Deltagere, der ansøger om ACHIEVE Master programmer (inkl. FSTP ansøgere) er forpligtet til at give visse personlige oplysninger til ansøgningssystemet (dvs. kontaktoplysninger, ID, e-mail og postadresser). Alle ansøgere skal læse og acceptere EIT Digital Privacy Policy og Master School vilkår og betingelser og samtykke til at tillade EIT Digital at gemme og behandle deres personlige oplysninger. Den dokumentation, der skal læses, forklarer årsagerne til behandlingen af personoplysninger, formålet med indsamlingen af personoplysninger og de foranstaltninger, der er truffet for at beskytte personoplysninger.

Yderligere oplysninger om FSTP-ansøgernes dataindsamling og -opbevaring findes i ACHIEVE's dataforvaltningsplan.

I henhold til GDPR har alle FSTP-ansøgere ret til: Få adgang til, rette eller slette deres data eller tilbagetrække samtykke til enhver tid. For at udøve disse rettigheder kan ansøgerne omdirigere deres anmodning til: privacy@28digital.eu

grants.title: Euroopa päev 2026 Narva
grants.description:
Organizing Europe Day concert in Narva, 9 May 2026
The EC Rep in Tallinn celebrates Europe Day in two cities in Estonia – Narva and Tallinn. Narva is the border city adjacent to Russia, located in the Ida-Viru county – the just transition area in Estonia, with a mainly Russian-speaking population who still celebrate 9 May as Soviet/Russian victory day.
To shift this narrative, the EC Representation, in cooperation with partners (such as the Government Office, the Ministry of Culture, the Integration Foundation, the Europe Direct Center in Narva, Narva Museum, the University of Tartu’s Narva College, and other local institutions), has for the past two years provided a solid and credible alternative: a big public concert on 9 May with A-list performers specially loved by local people in Ida-Viru region. The aim is to strengthen the narrative of peace over victory by placing European values and voices at the heart of the 9 May public sphere. 
The concert (and its’ side events) has been a huge success with at least the same amount of people attending the Europe Day celebrations than those visiting the propaganda events on the other side of the river banks in Narva. Therefore, it is of utmost importance to continue with the started tradition and consolidate even further the understanding of Europe Day and the celebration of peace in Narva. Europe Day celebrations help to deliver our common European message: Europe starts in Narva, and Europe Day belongs to all of us, regardless of background.
As such, in order to celebrate Europe Day on 9 May 2025, the EC Rep in Estonia is planning to organize a public concert in Narva, Estonia.

to shift this narrative, the ec representation, in cooperation with partners (such as the government office, the ministry of culture, the integration foundation, the europe direct center in narva, narva museum, the university of tartu’s narva college, and other local institutions), has for the past two years provided a solid and credible alternative: a big public concert on 9 may with a-list performers specially loved by local people in ida-viru region. the aim is to strengthen the narrative of peace over victory by placing european values and voices at the heart of the 9 may public sphere.

grants.title: Horizon Scanning System for Medical Devices & In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices
grants.description: The subject of this call for tenders is to set up a horizon scanning system for medical devices and in vitro diagnostic medical devices in order to maintain an up-to-date overview of new and emerging technologies. The system, based on an IT tool, will have to continuously perform screening of available sources for identification of novel, emerging technologies, and assess features that may have an impact on medical devices and in vitro diagnostic medical devices, present opportunities, risks or other features related to new and emerging technologies. The need for this horizon scanning system has been identified in the context of the implementation of the MDR/IVDR legislation in order to support competitiveness and innovation in the EU market, while ensuring a high level of protection of health for patients and users.

grants.title: Consultancy services for firearms shooting instructor’s course delivery, revision and providing individualised feedback for learners
grants.description:
Frontex intends to launch a NP 3 procurement procedure for the provision of specialized consultancy services to support the implementation of firearms shooting instructors training activities.

The objective of the assignment is to provide expert support in planning and conducting firearms instructors course, designing course requirements and drills to design firearms instructors firearms handling and content delivery abilities, contributing to the further development of the course curriculum.

The consultant will also be expected to deliver a high-level master class for advanced instructors and prepare final reports and recommendations for the Activity Manager.

The services are expected to be implemented over several short missions, including preparation and reporting.

The forthcoming tender will be open to qualified individual experts or organizations with proven experience in:

•	High level firearms training delivery
•	Firearms training design (including developing training concepts and training manuals).
•	Firearms instructor development and adult learning methodologies.
•	Development of personal development plans.

grants.title: EL ja Aafrika Liit – kliimaneutraalsete, sotsiaalselt õiglaste ja õiglaste toidusüsteemide suunas
grants.description:
Valitud projektid käivitatakse 2026. aasta novembris ja nende rakendamise tähtaeg on 12 kuud (kuni 2027. aasta oktoobrini). Rakendusperiood jaguneb kolmeks etapiks:

  1. etapp – projekteerimine (3 kuud / november 2026 – jaanuar 2027): 20 % kogutoetuse maksmine [Tuleb täita: tegevuskava];
  2. etapp – arendamine (6 kuud / veebruar 2027 – juuli 2027): 50 % maksmine kogutoetusest [Tulemuste tutvustamine];
  3. etapp – valideerimine (3 kuud / august 2027 – oktoober 2027): lõpliku 30 % maksmine kogutoetusest [Tulemus: aruanne levitamistegevuse ja saadud kogemuste kohta].

Kõigi valitud projektide puhul on maksed tulemuspõhised, mis tähendab, et alltoetused peavad makse saamiseks esitama pärast iga etapi lõppu eriaruande (eelrahastamist ei ole ette nähtud).

grants.title: Euratom-Adhoc-2021–2027
grants.description:
Tento podpůrný balíček má usnadnit studentům MSc v jaderné syntéze, kteří chtějí jít do zahraničí na stáž, a tím stimulovat kvalitu vzdělávání prostřednictvím výměn. Stáž se může uskutečnit ve výzkumném středisku pro jadernou syntézu nebo na univerzitě zapojené do výzkumu jaderné syntézy. Toto místo není omezeno pouze na Evropu. Studenti registrovaní na univerzitách, které jsou členy sítě FuseNet a/nebo vykonávají svou stáž u hostitelů, kteří jsou členy sítě FuseNet, a jejich domovská univerzita se nachází v jednom z členských států EUROfusion, mohou požádat o finanční podporu na svou stáž zde.

Finanční podpora se skládá z pevně stanovené částky podpory na cestu (v závislosti na vzdálenosti cesty) a podpory na ubytování/život (v závislosti na délce stáže). Přihlášku je třeba podat nejméně 2 týdny před zahájením stáže a do 6 měsíců od jejího zahájení. Je třeba upřesnit další zdroje financování stáže (stipendia nebo granty, náhrady nebo plat od hostitelského institutu, finanční prostředky z jiných režimů podpory EU atd.). Financování může být rovněž omezeno v případech, kdy byly finanční prostředky od společnosti FuseNet získány v minulosti (viz podmínky na internetových stránkách společnosti FuseNet).

grants.title: Quantum Technologies in Defence (Action Plan and Foundational Research for Quantum Technologies in Defence)
grants.description:
In the context of Technology and Innovation, EDA intends to launch a tender to develop an Action Plan and Foundational Research for Quantum Technologies in Defence. There will be 2 LOTS: 

LOT 1: Development and Implementation of the Action plan on Quantum: This lot aims to manage administrative aspects of the ‘Quantum Action Plan’ to be developed  for the RTI (Research Technology & Innovation) directorate, propose and consult experts, organize workshops, assist EDA Project Officers in general management of tasks, propose a structure for the Quantum Action Plan, draft the Action Plan and its annexes including roadmap(s) proposal(s) ; 

LOT 2: Launching Quantum Defence Projects for Foundational Research and Technology: Under this lot economic operators will provide services to identify, propose, and develop promising technologies to different CapTechs or within cross CapTechs activities. The objective is to support the initiation or continuation of R&T projects, and to act as an incubator for early-stage innovations. This could include supporting the first steps of EDA OB studies, Cat. B projects and/or EDF projects or the transition from EDA OB studies to Cat. B projects and/or EDF projects and propose next steps including roadmaps and demonstrations.

As part of the forthcoming tender, EDA is organizing an information session on 07 May 2025.

quantum technologies in defence (action plan and foundational research for quantum technologies in defence) in the context of technology and innovation, eda intends to launch a tender to develop an action plan and foundational research for quantum technologies in defence. there will be 2 lots: 

lot 1: development and implementation of the action plan on quantum: this lot aims to manage administrative aspects of the ‘quantum action plan’ to be developed  for the rti (research technology & innovation) directorate, propose and consult experts, organize workshops, assist eda project officers in general management of tasks, propose a structure for the quantum action plan, draft the action plan and its annexes including roadmap(s) proposal(s) ; 

lot 2: launching quantum defence projects for foundational research and technology: under this lot economic operators will provide services to identify, propose, and develop promising technologies to different captechs or within cross captechs activities. the objective is to support the initiation or continuation of r&t projects, and to act as an incubator for early-stage innovations. this could include supporting the first steps of eda ob studies, cat. b projects and/or edf projects or the transition from eda ob studies to cat. b projects and/or edf projects and propose next steps including roadmaps and demonstrations.

as part of the forthcoming tender, eda is organizing an information session on 07 may 2025.

grants.title: Appui à la gestion environnementale et sociale d’un projet de la STEG en Tunisie: assistance et conseil pour la mise en œuvre du CGES et CPR et suivi des EIES et PAR
grants.description: Le gouvernement de la République tunisienne, par l'intermédiaire du promoteur (STEG), a demandé à la BEI de financer son programme de réhabilitation du réseau de distribution (le «Projet») pour la période 2020-2025, dans le but de mettre à niveau et de moderniser son réseau MT/BT. Pour la Tunisie, l'objectif général du Projet est de renforcer le système national de transport et de distribution d'électricité, pour une meilleure couverture de la demande en électricité. Pour la STEG, l'objectif est de réduire les pertes techniques et commerciales et d'améliorer la fiabilité de la fourniture d'électricité dans sept zones de distribution (Centre, Grand Tunis, Nord, Nord-Ouest, Sud, Sud-Ouest, Sfax). Enfin, l'objectif général de l'AT est: 1) d'aligner la mise en œuvre du projet sur les normes environnementales et sociales (E&S) de la BEI et; 2) d'aider et soutenir la STEG dans la mise en œuvre de ce projet.

grants.title: Advertisement campaign, Europe Day 2026
grants.description:
The Representation of the European Commission in Estonia intends to purchase a comprehensive advertising campaign to promote the activities of Europe Day 2026. The campaign will focus primarily on the two key cities – Tallinn and Narva – where the largest events organized by the EC Representation will take place.
We are seeking to commission a campaign that covers the full spectrum of promotional channels in order to achieve the widest possible reach. This includes outdoor advertising, radio spots, online advertising, targeted campaigns on social media platforms, content marketing, and – where possible – television advertising. The goal is to ensure that the visibility and awareness of Europe Day 2026 extend broadly across society, engaging diverse audiences and highlighting the values of peace and unity that the day represents.
In May 2026, Estonia will once again celebrate Europe Day with large-scale public events in Tallinn and Narva, emphasizing 9 May as a day of peace and unity in Europe. Building on the success of previous years, the program highlights both festive and educational activities designed to reach diverse audiences across the country.
In Tallinn, the celebrations will open with an engaging orienteering game around the city, the announcement of the “European of the Year.” The central event, “Our Estonia, Our Europe,” will take place on Freedom Square, where EU embassies and related organizations will present exhibitions and activities. Visitors will be able to explore European cultures and take part in interactive programs. The day will culminate in a free evening concert at Freedom Square, broadcast live on ETV and ETV+, bringing together well-known performers to symbolize unity through music.
In Narva, Europe Day will continue to serve as a meaningful alternative to festivities across the river. The main concert on Town Hall Square will feature beloved artists and local school groups, attracting residents of Narva, Ida-Viru County, and guests from further afield. The University of Tartu Narva College will again host an international language café or Just Transition Fund job fair (tbc) with the participation of foreign embassies. Alongside the concerts, visitors can join an orienteering game, explore the EU information tent, and enjoy the café area.
The overall aim of Europe Day 2026 is to strengthen the narrative of peace over victory, ensuring that European values and voices are at the heart of the public sphere on 9 May. Events are organized in cooperation with the European Commission Representation, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the Government Office, the Ministry of Culture, the Integration Foundation, and local Europe Direct centers across Estonia.

grants.title: ZAG 2026 Zamjena uredskih tepiha
grants.description:
Ponuda treba sadržavati sljedeće stavke:
1.Demontaža postojeće podne obloge od tepih ploča postavljenih na uzdignutom modularnom podu, s u cijenu uključenim strojnim brušenjem i čiščenjem postojeće podne podloge, te odnos i odvoz svog otpadnog materijala na gradsku deponiju. 
2. Demontaža postojećeg podne lajsne, u cijenu treba biti uključen odnos i odvoz svog otpadnog materijala na gradsku deponiju.
3. Tekstilna podna obloga u pločama
4.Dobava i postava zidne kutne letvice (sokla), debljine 7mm, izrađene od tepih ploča sa tipskim pvc
završetkom.
5. Izmještanje uredske opreme, stolova, stolica i ormara.

grants.title: Horizon Results Booster-II -puitesopimus
grants.description: Tämän puitesopimuksen tavoitteena on sellaisten palvelujen tarjoaminen, joilla tuetaan tutkimustulosten levittämistä, hyödyntämistä ja arvostusta seuraaville tahoille:— EU:n rahoittamien T&I-hankkeiden edunsaajat, jotta heitä voidaan edelleen tukea levitys- ja hyödyntämissopimusvelvoitteidensa edistämisessä ja niiden ylittämisessä. Palvelujen avulla heille tarjotaan neuvoja ja tukea, jotta varmistaan, että heidän tuloksensa otetaan käyttöön asianomaisilla ”markkinoilla” ja/tai T&I-ekosysteemeissä, mikä johtaa vaikutusten luomiseen ja luovuuteen; ja— komissio käytäntöjen ja/tai toiminnallisten tavoitteiden tukemiseksi. Palvelut keskittyvät toimintoihin, jotka rakentavat ja/tai vahvistavat olemassa olevia levitys- ja hyödyntämisvalmiuksia ja helpottavat T&I-ekosysteemin vaikutusten syntymisen seurantaa.

grants.title: Copernicus SESA Hovering Horizon Magazine
grants.description:
The SatCen plans to award a framework contract in cascade (with a maximum of 2 economic operators) for the editing and production of the Copernicus SESA Hovering Horizon Magazine, the bimonthly magazine of the Copernicus Service in Support to EU External and Security Actions.

The Hovering Horizon magazine contains a reader’s digest of the most relevant advances in the field of earth observation that concern the realm of security. It deals with the main research and innovation events in the overlap between the worlds of space, science and security.

grants.description:
